notes: Guardrail applied to HYP-005 (Multi-Signal Threat): insufficient corroborating evidence.

safety_controls.description: Hypotheses with fewer than 2 evidence items are downgraded below formal reporting threshold.
Only hypotheses with confidence >= 0.6 are materialized as findings.
Potentially high-impact findings and thinly corroborated claims are flagged for analyst validation before operational use.
Hypotheses record whether evidence came from limited or multiple analytic sources.

investigation_limitations: This investigation uses network-derived evidence only and has no host-level telemetry.
Some detections rely on protocol metadata and heuristics rather than full semantic reconstruction.
Encrypted traffic may reduce visibility into true intent or content.
Threshold-based logic can reduce false positives but may also reduce recall.

hypotheses.title: Suspicious DNS Activity
hypotheses.description: High-entropy or unusually structured DNS queries suggest possible algorithmic domains, covert DNS use, or DNS-based command-and-control. Additional corroboration is required before classifying as tunneling.
false_positive_risks: High-entropy DNS can also appear in CDNs, telemetry, security products, and benign service-generated domains.
Repeated subdomain variation is suspicious but does not alone prove DNS tunneling.
missed_detection_risks: Low-volume DNS covert channels may stay below threshold.
Benign-looking domains used by attackers may evade entropy-based heuristics.
limitations: DNS classification relies on metadata and naming patterns rather than payload semantics.

hypotheses.title: Suspicious TLS Session
hypotheses.description: Suspicious TLS metadata suggests encrypted malicious communication.
false_positive_risks: Missing SNI may occur in privacy-focused or legacy environments and is not inherently malicious.
TLS on non-standard ports is suspicious but can still be legitimate.
missed_detection_risks: If JA3 is unavailable, TLS detections rely on weaker metadata signals.
Encrypted malicious traffic with normal TLS fingerprints may not be flagged.
limitations: TLS analysis may be constrained by unavailable JA3, limited SNI visibility, or incomplete handshake metadata.

hypotheses.title: External Sensitive Access
hypotheses.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
missed_detection_risks: Access via VPN tunnels that terminate internally will not appear as external.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

hypotheses.title: Potential Data Exfiltration
hypotheses.description: Large or frequent outbound transfers to external host suggest data exfiltration.
false_positive_risks: Large legitimate uploads (backups, cloud sync, CI/CD) may trigger volumetric thresholds.
missed_detection_risks: Slow, low-volume exfiltration may stay below detection thresholds.
Encrypted exfiltration via legitimate services may not be flagged.
limitations: Volumetric analysis detects transfer patterns, not content — payload inspection requires decryption.

hypotheses.title: Multi-Signal Threat
hypotheses.description: Multiple suspicious communication patterns from the same host suggest coordinated malicious activity.
false_positive_risks: A host with multiple benign anomalies may still be flagged by correlation.
missed_detection_risks: Attacks using only one communication channel will not trigger multi-signal correlation.
limitations: Correlation strength depends on the quality and coverage of upstream analyzers.

findings.title: External Sensitive Access
findings.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
findings.recommendation: Verify authorization of external access, reset credentials on accessed hosts, and review for signs of post-exploitation activity.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
missed_detection_risks: Access via VPN tunnels that terminate internally will not appear as external.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

findings.title: Suspicious TLS Session
findings.description: Suspicious TLS metadata suggests encrypted malicious communication.
findings.recommendation: Review certificate, SNI, JA3, and destination context; block suspicious encrypted channels pending verification.
false_positive_risks: Missing SNI may occur in privacy-focused or legacy environments and is not inherently malicious.
TLS on non-standard ports is suspicious but can still be legitimate.
missed_detection_risks: If JA3 is unavailable, TLS detections rely on weaker metadata signals.
Encrypted malicious traffic with normal TLS fingerprints may not be flagged.
limitations: TLS analysis may be constrained by unavailable JA3, limited SNI visibility, or incomplete handshake metadata.

findings.title: Suspicious DNS Activity
findings.description: High-entropy or unusually structured DNS queries suggest possible algorithmic domains, covert DNS use, or DNS-based command-and-control. Additional corroboration is required before classifying as tunneling.
findings.recommendation: Investigate flagged domains, check threat intelligence, and block confirmed malicious domains.
false_positive_risks: High-entropy DNS can also appear in CDNs, telemetry, security products, and benign service-generated domains.
Repeated subdomain variation is suspicious but does not alone prove DNS tunneling.
missed_detection_risks: Low-volume DNS covert channels may stay below threshold.
Benign-looking domains used by attackers may evade entropy-based heuristics.
limitations: DNS classification relies on metadata and naming patterns rather than payload semantics.

findings.title: Potential Data Exfiltration
findings.description: Large or frequent outbound transfers to external host suggest data exfiltration.
findings.recommendation: Block the destination IP, isolate the source host, and forensically examine what data may have been transferred.
false_positive_risks: Large legitimate uploads (backups, cloud sync, CI/CD) may trigger volumetric thresholds.
missed_detection_risks: Slow, low-volume exfiltration may stay below detection thresholds.
Encrypted exfiltration via legitimate services may not be flagged.
limitations: Volumetric analysis detects transfer patterns, not content — payload inspection requires decryption.
